document_pack.title: Lloyd's catastrophe claims guidance for U.S. property losses
document_pack.why_it_matters: Provides catastrophe-adjusting context relevant to timing, documentation, and delegated-claims handling.

document_pack.title: Louisiana Department of Insurance Hurricane Ida claims data call
document_pack.why_it_matters: Shows regulator focus on carrier responsiveness and claim-volume handling after Ida.

document_pack.title: Louisiana policyholder suit over Lloyd's Hurricane Ida adjustment
document_pack.why_it_matters: Surfaces recurring dispute themes around flood attribution, delay, and under-scoping after Ida.

common_defenses: Flood exclusion - wind vs. water causation dispute
Late notice / failure to mitigate defense
rebuttal_angles: Key fact undermines carrier position: Hurricane-force winds were documented in Orleans Parish during Ida landfall.
Key fact undermines carrier position: Interior water intrusion was reported immediately after roof and window-envelope damage.
Counter flood-causation defense: contemporaneous wind reporting supports a covered wind-driven opening before later water intrusion.
Potential bad-faith signal: investigate Lloyd's adjusting timeline, engineer retention, and scope revisions after the initial inspection.
